title: senecio madrasensis
intro: Senecio madrasensis, also known as the Madras groundsel, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to India and parts of Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing. In herbal preparations, Senecio madrasensis is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or ointments, and is also used as an ingredient in some traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicines.
